steps: Let n be the initial number of books. The student borrows 3/4 of them, so books borrowed = (3/4)n. Books left on shelf = n - (3/4)n = (1/4)n. Then the student returns 5 books, so books on shelf become (1/4)n + 5. This equals 17. So (1/4)n + 5 = 17. Subtract 5: (1/4)n = 12. Multiply by 4: n = 48. Check: 48 books initially, borrow 3/4 = 36, left 12, return 5 gives 17. Correct.
